notYetAutomatedInPlaywright.name: Advanced Logic popup (the big “fx” / expression editor)
notYetAutomatedInPlaywright.howWeTestToday: Now covered by a dedicated **domain/property/component** stack plus one focused **Playwright** flow. The full combinatorial surface is still broader than browser automation alone, so the **Test catalog** remains the backlog for additional equivalence classes.
notYetAutomatedInPlaywright.why: The popup spans parser, evaluator, theme inheritance, token assignment, and dense UI editing. We automate this mostly below the browser layer for scale and determinism, then keep a smaller E2E slice for wiring confidence.

notYetAutomatedInPlaywright.name: Token table popup, publish flow, cloud sync
notYetAutomatedInPlaywright.howWeTestToday: Partially touched by token-operations and project specs; deep paths are mostly **manual** in the catalog.
notYetAutomatedInPlaywright.why: Requires stable test accounts or mocked backends for full coverage.

introForNonTechnicalReaders: This screen shows what the **automated robots** actually checked in this run. **Unit** tests exercise tiny pieces of code in milliseconds. **E2E** (end-to-end) tests open a real browser like you would and click through flows. They are only a **slice** of quality: the full **Test catalog** tab lists many more scenarios (including manual checks) that are not all automated yet.
catalogVsAutomation: The **Test catalog** is the master list of what *should* be verified for the product. **This run** is only what the computer executed automatically. A passing run does not mean every catalog line is covered — it means every **listed row below** passed.

slugify.unit.test.ts.fileBlurb: Validates helper logic that turns project titles into URL slugs and finds a project when you open a link.

plainByPhrase.includes: lowercases and replaces spaces
plainByPhrase.plain: A normal title like “My Project” becomes a clean web-style id: my-project.

plainByPhrase.includes: strips accents
plainByPhrase.plain: Letters with accents (e.g. café) become plain letters so URLs stay predictable.

plainByPhrase.includes: returns untitled for empty-ish
plainByPhrase.plain: Garbage-only titles fall back to “untitled” so the app never produces an empty slug.

plainByPhrase.includes: resolves by slugified name
plainByPhrase.plain: When you have a slug derived from a name, the app can find the right project.

plainByPhrase.includes: falls back to id match
plainByPhrase.plain: If someone uses the raw project id in the URL, it still resolves correctly.

slugify.unit.test.ts.fallbackPlain: Unit check for slug / lookup helper behavior.

app-navigation.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Opens the app like a user and checks that main URLs work and the browser’s Back button does not break the screen.

plainByPhrase.includes: loads without uncaught page errors
plainByPhrase.plain: Home loads without hidden JavaScript crashes in the background.

plainByPhrase.includes: home resolves to editor or projects dashboard
plainByPhrase.plain: Opening “/” lands you either in the editor or the projects list — something real should appear.

plainByPhrase.includes: /projects shows projects page
plainByPhrase.plain: The /projects address shows your projects screen and list area.

plainByPhrase.includes: /sample-project shows editor shell
plainByPhrase.plain: The sample project route opens the editor shell and toolbar.

plainByPhrase.includes: browser back navigation does not blank screen
plainByPhrase.plain: Going from projects → sample → Back returns to projects without a blank page.

project-management.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Creates a local project from the UI and checks it appears on the dashboard; also checks import entry points.

plainByPhrase.includes: create local project opens editor
plainByPhrase.plain: Creating a new local project drops you into the editor with toolbar and canvas.

plainByPhrase.includes: project card appears after create
plainByPhrase.plain: After creating a project, going back to the list shows at least as many cards as before.

plainByPhrase.includes: import button is available on projects page
plainByPhrase.plain: The projects page shows a way to import a project file.

sample-templates.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Smoke check that built-in sample routes still render the editor.

plainByPhrase.includes: sample route loads editor shell
plainByPhrase.plain: The sample route still opens the editor shell (template/demo path works).

persistence.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Checks that work saved locally survives refreshing the browser tab.

plainByPhrase.includes: local project survives reload
plainByPhrase.plain: After you work in a local project, reloading the page keeps your project usable (data did not vanish).

import-export.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Sanity checks for bringing data in and copying or downloading code output.

plainByPhrase.includes: projects page exposes import
plainByPhrase.plain: Projects screen exposes import so you can bring work in from a file.

plainByPhrase.includes: code preview has copy and download
plainByPhrase.plain: Code view offers copy and download so you can take generated output out of the app.

node-operations.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Inside the editor, adds nodes from the toolbar and opens related panels.

plainByPhrase.includes: add HSL root node from toolbar
plainByPhrase.plain: You can add an HSL color node from the bottom “add node” menu and see it on the canvas.

plainByPhrase.includes: add palette node
plainByPhrase.plain: You can add a palette node and see it appear on the canvas.

plainByPhrase.includes: open token table from toolbar
plainByPhrase.plain: Toolbar can open the token table popup and close it again.

undo-redo.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Checks history: undo removes the last change; redo brings it back.

plainByPhrase.includes: undo removes last node after add
plainByPhrase.plain: After adding a node, Undo removes that last addition.

plainByPhrase.includes: redo restores after undo
plainByPhrase.plain: After Undo, Redo puts the change back.

token-operations.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Tokens panel: visibility, adding a variable, and search filtering.

plainByPhrase.includes: tokens panel container and search visible
plainByPhrase.plain: Tokens panel shows its container and a search field so you can find tokens.

plainByPhrase.includes: add variable creates token row
plainByPhrase.plain: Adding a design token variable creates a new row you can see.

plainByPhrase.includes: token search filters
plainByPhrase.plain: Typing in token search narrows the list.

themes-pages.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Toolbar menus for switching pages and themes, and adding a page.

plainByPhrase.includes: page dropdown opens
plainByPhrase.plain: The page dropdown in the toolbar opens so you can switch pages.

plainByPhrase.includes: theme dropdown opens
plainByPhrase.plain: The theme dropdown opens so you can change theme.

plainByPhrase.includes: add new page from toolbar
plainByPhrase.plain: You can add a new page from the toolbar flow.

command-palette.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Command palette opens from the UI and keyboard, closes with Escape, and search filters results.

plainByPhrase.includes: open via bottom bar and close with Escape
plainByPhrase.plain: Palette opens from the bottom bar and Escape closes it.

plainByPhrase.includes: open via keyboard shortcut
plainByPhrase.plain: Keyboard shortcut opens the command palette.

plainByPhrase.includes: search filters results area
plainByPhrase.plain: Typing in the palette filters what you see in the results list.

keyboard-shortcuts.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Keyboard shortcuts for palette, code view, and the shortcuts help panel.

plainByPhrase.includes: Cmd/Ctrl+K opens command palette
plainByPhrase.plain: Cmd/Ctrl+K opens the command palette.

plainByPhrase.includes: switch to code view with toolbar
plainByPhrase.plain: You can switch to code view using the toolbar without breaking the layout.

plainByPhrase.includes: shortcuts panel toggles
plainByPhrase.plain: The shortcuts help panel can be opened and closed.

ai-chat.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Checks how the AI entry behaves for local vs cloud/template contexts (expectations differ).

plainByPhrase.includes: AI button visible for cloud/template only
plainByPhrase.plain: AI affordances differ by project type: cloud/template vs local — the test asserts the expected visibility or messaging.

a11y.audit.ts.fileBlurb: Serious/critical accessibility smoke coverage for the projects dashboard and the visible Advanced popup state.

plainByPhrase.includes: projects dashboard has no serious or critical accessibility violations
plainByPhrase.plain: The projects dashboard does not expose serious or critical accessibility issues in its visible state.

plainByPhrase.includes: advanced popup has no serious or critical accessibility violations in its visible state
plainByPhrase.plain: The visible Advanced popup state does not expose serious or critical accessibility issues.

advanced-logic-engine.domain.test.ts.fileBlurb: Pure evaluator coverage for parser behavior, constraints, theme resolution, token assignment, export paths, and golden fixtures.

advanced-logic-engine.property.test.ts.fileBlurb: Seeded generated checks that run many valid Advanced Logic combinations without relying on brittle browser clicks.

color-conversions.domain.test.ts.fileBlurb: Checks color conversion helpers directly, including HEX formatting, HSL/RGB round-trips, OKLCH stability, and gamut clamping.

plainByPhrase.includes: formats RGB values as uppercase hex and clamps out-of-range channels
plainByPhrase.plain: RGB channels are clamped into a valid range and exported as uppercase hex.

plainByPhrase.includes: round-trips canonical RGB values through HSL exactly or within a one-step tolerance
plainByPhrase.plain: Core RGB colors survive an RGB to HSL to RGB round-trip without noticeable drift.

plainByPhrase.includes: keeps muted HSL colors reasonably stable through OKLCH
plainByPhrase.plain: Muted colors stay visually close when converted through OKLCH and back.

plainByPhrase.includes: keeps neutral colors stable when round-tripping through OKLCH
plainByPhrase.plain: Neutral grayscale colors remain neutral after an OKLCH round-trip.

plainByPhrase.includes: clamps out-of-gamut OKLCH values into valid sRGB output
plainByPhrase.plain: Extreme OKLCH values are safely pulled back into displayable RGB output.

hct-utils.domain.test.ts.fileBlurb: Checks HCT utility helpers for normalization, hex parsing, RGB conversion, and max-chroma behavior.

plainByPhrase.includes: normalizes hue and clamps chroma/tone before converting
plainByPhrase.plain: Invalid HCT inputs are normalized into safe hue, chroma, and tone values before conversion.

plainByPhrase.includes: round-trips HCT-derived RGB values back into close HCT readings
plainByPhrase.plain: Colors converted out of HCT and back stay close to the original HCT reading.

plainByPhrase.includes: treats hex input with or without a hash consistently
plainByPhrase.plain: Hex parsing behaves the same whether the input includes the hash prefix or not.

plainByPhrase.includes: agrees with rgbToHex when converting through HCT to RGB
plainByPhrase.plain: HCT conversion stays consistent with the standard RGB-to-hex formatter.

plainByPhrase.includes: reports finite max chroma values within the supported gamut
plainByPhrase.plain: The max-chroma helper returns sane finite values within the expected gamut.

visibility.domain.test.ts.fileBlurb: Checks hidden-node and hidden-token rules, including palette inheritance and theme-specific filtering.

plainByPhrase.includes: toggles visibility per theme and cleans up empty maps
plainByPhrase.plain: Turning visibility on and off only changes the selected theme, and empty maps are removed.

plainByPhrase.includes: treats palette shades as hidden when their parent palette is hidden in the same theme
plainByPhrase.plain: A hidden parent palette hides its child shades for that same theme.

plainByPhrase.includes: treats token visibility as explicit, forced-by-node, and owner-hidden
plainByPhrase.plain: Tokens can be hidden directly, by hidden assigned nodes, or by a hidden token-node owner.

plainByPhrase.includes: filters visible tokens without leaking hidden node or owner-hidden tokens
plainByPhrase.plain: The visible-token filter only keeps tokens that still have a visible path through the theme.

computed-tokens.domain.test.ts.fileBlurb: Resolves exported tokens across themes, including alias chains, hidden propagation, and project isolation.

plainByPhrase.includes: computes a visible color token with stable export metadata
plainByPhrase.plain: A visible color token is exported with stable names, page path, and color fields.

plainByPhrase.includes: resolves token-node value refs from the active theme and falls back to primary
plainByPhrase.plain: Token-node exports use the active theme value token first and fall back to the primary theme when needed.

plainByPhrase.includes: omits hidden palette-shade and token-node owner tokens from the computed outputs
plainByPhrase.plain: Hidden palette shades and hidden token-node owners do not appear in the computed export list.

plainByPhrase.includes: keeps project-level computations isolated when computing all projects
plainByPhrase.plain: Computing all projects keeps each project’s token output separate.

AdvancedPopup.component.test.tsx.fileBlurb: Component-level coverage for the Advanced Popup selectors and layouts used by automation.

useAdvancedLogicEffect.integration.test..fileBlurb: Store/effect integration coverage for applying Advanced Logic and synchronizing assigned tokens.

advanced-logic.spec.ts.fileBlurb: Real-browser coverage for opening the Advanced Popup, entering a simple hue rule, saving it, and reopening it.

plainByPhrase.includes: save and reopen a simple hue expression from the advanced popup
plainByPhrase.plain: A user can open the Advanced popup, save a simple hue rule, close it, and reopen it without losing the result.
